summary: Completed the matching C decompilation for Mewtwo's Up-B Teleport by adding src/melee/ft/chara/ftMewtwo/ftMewtwo_SpecialHi.c, removing the corresponding assembly from asm/melee/ft/chara/ftmewtwo.s, wiring the new object into obj_files.mk, and expanding Mewtwo headers/statevars to expose Teleport action IDs, attributes, callbacks, and per-move state. The PR also normalized several fighter state-var field names across Ness, Luigi, Mario, Game & Watch, Captain Falcon, and common cliff code to lower-case move names, and added reusable ledge-facing macros in fighter.h. There were no review comments in the slice; the PR body only notes this was a cleaned-up fork replacing an older long-history PR.
what_changed: Added a 632-line matching C implementation for Mewtwo Teleport covering start, travel/zoom, and lost/end phases for both grounded and aerial variants.
Removed roughly 1,200 lines of Mewtwo assembly from asm/melee/ft/chara/ftmewtwo.s and replaced it with the new C object in obj_files.mk.
Implemented Teleport GFX setup callbacks using x21BC_callback_Accessory4, x21D4_callback_EnterHitlag, and x21D8_callback_ExitHitlag, including start/end GFX handling and the x2219 effect-spawn flag.

Added Mewtwo Teleport action-state enum values such as AS_MEWTWO_SPECIALHI_START, AS_MEWTWO_SPECIALHI, AS_MEWTWO_SPECIALHI_LOST, AS_MEWTWO_SPECIALAIRHI_START, AS_MEWTWO_SPECIALAIRHI, and AS_MEWTWO_SPECIALAIRHI_LOST.

Documented and named many Mewtwo special attributes, especially Teleport values at x40-x74: velocity divisors, gravity, terminal velocity, duration, stick range, momentum, angle clamp, end momentum multiplier, freefall mobility, and landing lag.
Added ftMewtwo_StateVars.h with ftMewtwoSpecialHi fields for travelFrames, stickX, stickY, unk4 timer, velX, velY, and groundVelX, and exposed mewtwoVars[0] at Fighter offset 0x2340.
Added FTMEWTWO_SPECIALHI_COLL_FLAG as a combined action-state-change preservation/update flag set used for ground/air transitions.
Added CLIFFCATCH_O(fp) and CLIFFCATCH_U(fp) macros in fighter.h to express facing-direction-dependent ledge checks.
Renamed Mewtwo's ftMewTwoAttributes type to ftMewtwoAttributes and renamed x2234 to x2234_shadowBallCharge.
Changed Mewtwo item registration from raw numeric item IDs to It_Kind_Mewtwo_Disable and It_Kind_Mewtwo_ShadowBall.
Normalized state-var union member names in nearby fighter code from capitalized move names to lower-case/camel-case names, such as Ness AttackHi4 to attackHi4 and SpecialHi to specialHi.
smart_moves: Split the move into conventional fighter callback groups: StartAction, Anim, IASA, Phys, Coll, ground-to-air, air-to-ground, and Lost/End handlers, making the decomp easier to compare against other fighter special moves.
Introduced a dedicated ftMewtwoSpecialHi statevar struct instead of continuing to use raw 0x2340 offsets, preserving offset comments while improving readability.
Named special attributes from observed Teleport behavior, turning opaque x40-x74 fields into semantically useful constants for velocity, drift, landing lag, and momentum calculations.
Captured Teleport's velocity before zeroing during the Lost transition, then reapplied scaled momentum using x6C_MEWTWO_TELEPORT_MOMENTUM_END_MUL for ground and aerial end states.
Used inline helper functions for repeated setup logic: ftMewtwo_SpecialHi_SetVars and ftMewtwo_SpecialHiLost_SetVars reduce duplication across grounded and aerial variants.
Abstracted ledge-facing checks into CLIFFCATCH_O and CLIFFCATCH_U macros, which made Mewtwo collision code clearer and may help future collision-related matches.
Preserved matching-sensitive constants directly in C, including HALF_PI32, DEG_TO_RAD, 35.0f action-frame starts, 0.0010000000474974513f stick threshold, and action-state preserve flag combinations.
Left decomp.me scratch links and function address comments beside the new functions, which helps future auditors trace matching provenance.
decomp_lessons: For special-move decomp, introduce action IDs, statevars, and attribute names together with the C file; matching code becomes much easier to reason about when offsets are explained at the same time.
Grounded and aerial versions of the same move often share state setup but differ in physics/collision transitions; small helpers can preserve matching while avoiding duplicated C.
When replacing assembly with C, remember to update obj_files.mk so the new C object precedes or replaces the old asm object in the build sequence.
Use statevar unions at Fighter offset 0x2340 for per-character move state rather than raw field accesses; this PR demonstrates adding mewtwoVars alongside existing luigiVars and gameWatchVars.
Collision code can be made more readable with macros for common facing-direction ledge-catch ternaries, but separate macros may be needed for fcmpo versus fcmpu-sensitive codegen.
Large header churn may accompany matching work when previously anonymous fields become named; keep names behavior-based and include offset comments when certainty is limited.
If an attribute or state field is not understood, preserve uncertainty in the name/comment, as with x54_MEWTWO_TELEPORT_UNK2 and specialHi.unk4, rather than inventing precise semantics.
naming_conventions: Mewtwo type casing was standardized to ftMewtwoAttributes instead of ftMewTwoAttributes.
Mewtwo action enum values use AS_MEWTWO_SPECIALHI_* and AS_MEWTWO_SPECIALAIRHI_* names mirroring the action-state table.
Mewtwo Teleport attributes use xOFFSET_MEWTWO_TELEPORT_DESCRIPTION names, such as x50_MEWTWO_TELEPORT_DURATION and x74_MEWTWO_TELEPORT_LANDING_LAG.
Per-move statevars use lower-case/camel-case union members such as specialHi, attackHi4, attackLw4, specialN, and cliffCommon rather than capitalized move names.
Item IDs should prefer It_Kind_* enum names over raw numbers, demonstrated by It_Kind_Mewtwo_Disable and It_Kind_Mewtwo_ShadowBall.
Special move callbacks follow the fighter naming pattern ftCharacter_SpecialMovePhase_CallbackType, for example ftMewtwo_SpecialAirHiLost_Coll and ftMewtwo_SpecialHiStart_GroundToAir.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Replaced assembly labels beginning at 0x801450A0 with named C functions while preserving address comments for each decompiled routine.
Used exact floating-point constants and macros including HALF_PI32 and DEG_TO_RAD to match trigonometric movement and collision-angle checks.
Used Fighter_ActionStateChange_800693AC with carefully preserved flags, current animation frame, and anim-rate arguments to match ground/air transitions.
Kept matching-sensitive repeated assignments visible in places, such as repeated getFtSpecialAttrsD/getFighter patterns and temporary variables, rather than over-cleaning them.
Used fp->cb function-pointer assignments for effect spawning and hitlag pause/resume callbacks instead of directly running all visual effects in state handlers.
Encoded collision surface checks with x134_envFlags masks 0x3F, 0xFC0, and 0x6000, plus lbvector_AngleXY comparisons against the Teleport angle clamp.
Added CLIFFCATCH_O(fp) for fcmpo-based facing checks and CLIFFCATCH_U(fp) for fcmpu-based checks, indicating attention to codegen differences.
review_feedback: No review comments or formal reviews were present in the provided PR slice.
The PR body says: "Discard the old PR please, this is a 'new' fork I've created with the purpose of cleaning its notoriously long commit history."
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename or clarify ftMewtwoSpecialHi.unk4 or x54_MEWTWO_TELEPORT_UNK2.
Check whether later Mewtwo PRs refine the Teleport attribute comments or split ftmewtwo.h into more specific headers.
Search for subsequent uses or changes to CLIFFCATCH_O and CLIFFCATCH_U to confirm whether the macro distinction held up.
Look for PRs that further reduce raw collision envFlags masks 0x3F, 0xFC0, and 0x6000 into named constants.
Compare this Mewtwo Teleport implementation with Zelda/Fox/Falco up-special decomp patterns for reusable transition and momentum matching tactics.
